mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly increasing present value estimates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 5.0 percent signal labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive earnings revisions that drive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint regression coefficient for RSAFS and HOUST interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in the index of industrial production following a supply shock creates short-term momentum as firms ramp output and capacity utilization rises, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production rebounds are negative in > 50% of observations, or autocorrelation of returns breaks the momentum pattern.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 280.0 reflect entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or Rank IC for CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.05 in the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and unemployment rates below 4.5 percent confirm a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE yields negative predictive power, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding economic activity that translates directly into higher corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100.0 signals widespread manufacturing contraction and weak business investment, triggering risk aversion that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
